interactions.summary: Jiang extends bureaucratic bloat to federal departments and hospitals, arguing that administrators evaluate themselves, reward themselves, and make healthcare expensive by denying and complicating claims.
He uses Kafka's The Trial and a personal Toronto policing story to argue that bureaucrats target compliant ordinary people because that justifies their existence with less risk than doing real work.

claims.claim: Jiang says every federal bureaucracy shows a similar ratio problem: too many administrators and managers who do little, too few people doing science, engineering, and real work.
He argues that bureaucratic evaluation is rigged because managers evaluate themselves and their friends as outstanding while demanding more from workers.
Jiang says US healthcare costs are driven not by superior care but by exploding administrators who invent ways to deny or complicate claims.
Kafka's The Trial becomes Jiang's literary model for bureaucracy: a system arrests innocent people and wages pointless prosecutions because it needs activity even when everything is meaningless.
Jiang says bureaucrats prefer compliant innocent people over real criminals or harder problems because compliant people let bureaucrats justify themselves with minimal work.

signature_moments.moment: The bureaucracy does not need guilt; it needs innocent people it can process.
signature_moments.source_phrase: arrest innocent people and wage pointless prosecutions
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns Kafka into the lecture's model of administrative self-justification.

signature_moments.moment: Bureaucrats make problems for ordinary citizens because ordinary citizens are easier than real problems.
signature_moments.source_phrase: make problems for ordinary citizens because life becomes easier for them
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It links the personal police story back to the abstract bureaucracy theory.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Kafka novel Jiang uses as a model of a society where bureaucrats process innocent people because procedure has replaced meaning.
An ordinary person bureaucrats can pressure safely because the person will not fight back.

uncertainty_notes.note: A short ASR artifact appears in the Toronto story; the narrative remains intelligible around it.
